title: Pilosella schelkownikowii
taxonomy: Pilosella schelkownikowii belongs to the family Rosaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is a member of the genus Pilosella, which is part of the larger group of plants known as the tribe Cichorieae. Within the tribe, Pilosella schelkownikowii is classified as part of the subgenus Eunomo. The plant's classification is also closely related to that of the genus Cirsium. Further classification details of Pilosella schelkownikowii may be found in more specialized botanical sources.

intro: Pilosella schelkownikowii, also known as Russian fleabane, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella schelkownikowii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pilosella schelkownikowii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and promote overall well-being.
